title: Living Boulder Vlog 2-16-2017: Old Boulder in Background

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a vlog about his historical Boulder background images project, his plans to sell his car and start riding his bike indoors on rollers, and a persistent hamstring/leg pain caused by sharp edges on old furniture that is interrupting his ability to sit and work.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Knows he is good at abandoning projects to start new ones, accepts this about himself
Plans to ride bike on rollers while working at computer as a productive habit
Burned out on it — too big a project, set aside
Abandoned it quickly after realizing cobblestone/road mapping was too crazy

claims.subject: Boulder
claims.claim: The first building in Boulder was the post office in 1864.

claims.subject: Boulder
claims.claim: In 1887, there was a directory in the 1887 folder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle worked on a project in 2015 that was gutted and not completed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle spent eight minutes and 25 seconds working on a project showing Satakwa in 1915.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thought the project was the last one he would do, but it was too big and he cracked.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle discovered Rome and dropped everything to focus on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle tried to map cobblestones and roads in Rome but realized it was crazy and gave up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle started working on selling his car and his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to start riding his bike while working on the computer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle got a new couch because his old one was cutting his leg.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's old couch had a sharp edge that caused a permanent dent in his hamstring muscle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle experienced sharp shooting pains in his hamstring even after getting a new couch.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels sick and toxic all the time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is good at dropping stuff and starting something new.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to sell his car soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has been trying to get up early to work on his car but is struggling with his sleep rhythm.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has been lying in bed at night unable to sleep until sunrise.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks riding his bike might help him sleep at night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes selling his car will give him enough money to fix his room.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to ride his bike on rollers for the whole year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is close to finishing some projects and thinks he can be productive while riding his bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle needs to have his website ready before starting a show.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to make a webpage quickly to start the show.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's hamstring pain is so bad he can't sit down or work properly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is scared because the pain in his hamstring comes all the way up from his butt.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to shampoo tomorrow.

transcript.text: Hey, Living in Boulder vlog. It's set for February 16th. It's Thursday.
Alright, so let me do the view, talk about the background first.
Background today is Boulder, and the first one that I use is 1864, and the first building that was there, which is today Post Office, and then I go to 1868, and then an 1887 directory,
And then 1887 Boulder, and I go down to the Reservoir.
I think you can download this off my website, although I'll do some work. I'll look at my website pretty soon as soon as I'm done with my car.
So, and then it was 1898. It was gutted, the center part, because I had that in 1915. It was the same thing. Or at least what I have. I'm not done with it.
And then I did 1915, showing Chautauqua. And so, yeah, I was doing that for eight minutes and 25 seconds, kind of taking a while.
So, it's not done. What happened is, yeah, I burned out on it, you know, it's a big project.
I wanted to, I remember when I started it, I think it was last time, I was sure that that was the last project I was going to do, but it just, it was too big.
And so, I cracked, and then I started doing, like, touching up other things. And then, and then I discovered Rome, and I dropped everything, and I went after Rome.

And I went after Rome until I tried to make the cobblestones and all of the roads, and went in one day and realized that was nuts, and went crazy, and I was going to, like, ride my bike, start riding my bike.
And so, I set up my room, started to set up my room with that stuff. And then I started, I needed to work on selling my car and my website.
But, um, but I think I'm going to start riding my bike now, because, uh, I got a, I, I got an injury a couple weeks ago.
Um, what happened was, um, uh, in my old couch in my room, it's like a bucket couch.
Hmm. And the, um, I guess the foam kind of wore away towards the end, but it has, like, it's really sharp, it has this wood that comes up, and it was cutting on my leg.

Like, and then the chair in the living room, and my mom got me these three chairs, and so I had to throw one away, but my roommate wanted to keep his wooden one, so I had to throw my one, my comfortable one.
And I was sitting in, those ones weren't, and it was good, and I was sitting in those, and those were cutting on my leg, too, like, halfway up my hamstring, so.
Um, and so I began to, hmm, it was like I did a permanent dent in my hamstring muscle, because it was just, like, woods poking in it for so long.
Um, and so, and then I was getting, um, sharp shooting pains, sharp pain just right out of the hamstring out of nowhere, even though I have a new couch, which is nicer.
And, um, so I was, like, freaking out.
I'm really happy now.
But I was like, I mean, yeah, because it's not stopping.
Even after this new one, it's just continuing.

It's that I know how to drop stuff and start something new, you know.
